Released RTC base flow-matching checkpoint executed with naive asynchronous chunking (no test-time correction), reproduced in the Soft RTC paper (Liu et al. 2026, Table I). 12-task RTC protocol on the released large Kinetix levels (state-based). Reported solve rate is the overall mean averaged across inference delays d=0..4, not a single delay, so it is not directly comparable to per-delay (single-d) entries; stored in task_scores.reported_avg. Execution horizon s=4, 5 flow steps, 32 rollout episodes per level-delay tuple.

Inference-time RTC (Real-Time Chunking, Black et al. 2025, arXiv:2506.07339) — the original inference-time hard prefix-masking variant — applied to the released base checkpoint and reproduced in the Soft RTC paper (Liu et al. 2026, Table I). 12-task RTC protocol on the released large Kinetix levels (state-based). Reported solve rate is the overall mean averaged across inference delays d=0..4, not a single delay, so it is not directly comparable to per-delay (single-d) entries; stored in task_scores.reported_avg. Execution horizon s=4, 5 flow steps, 32 rollout episodes per level-delay tuple. Inference-time guidance costs ~2.6x naive runtime.

RTC (Real-Time Chunking, Black et al. 2025) reproduced by the REMAC paper under clean (no-corruption) delay conditions (Appendix F, Table 7). 12-task RTC protocol. Suite scores are mean success rate over the 12 tasks at inference delay d=0..4; for each d, execution horizon e is averaged within max(1,d) to 9-d. Scores at different d are not directly comparable.

Training-time RTC (Black et al. 2025, arXiv:2512.05964): one-epoch fine-tune of the released base checkpoint with simulated delay and a binary prefix (zero) schedule, reproduced in the Soft RTC paper (Liu et al. 2026, Table I, seed-0 operating point). 12-task RTC protocol on the released large Kinetix levels (state-based). Reported solve rate is the overall mean averaged across inference delays d=0..4, not a single delay, so it is not directly comparable to per-delay (single-d) entries; stored in task_scores.reported_avg. Execution horizon s=4, 5 flow steps, 32 rollout episodes per level-delay tuple. Near-naive runtime.

Soft RTC (action-prior denoising) applied at inference time on the released base checkpoint with no fine-tuning (Liu et al. 2026, Table I). 12-task RTC protocol on the released large Kinetix levels (state-based). Reported solve rate is the overall mean averaged across inference delays d=0..4, not a single delay, so it is not directly comparable to per-delay (single-d) entries; stored in task_scores.reported_avg. Execution horizon s=4, 5 flow steps, 32 rollout episodes per level-delay tuple. Inference-time guidance costs ~2.7x naive runtime.

Soft RTC (action-prior denoising), training-time variant: one-epoch fine-tune of the released base checkpoint with delay-scaled soft-conditioning (h_max=5, lambda=2, linear schedule). N=1 is the performance-preserving operating point, nearly matching Train Hard RTC (Liu et al. 2026, Table I). 12-task RTC protocol on the released large Kinetix levels (state-based). Reported solve rate is the overall mean averaged across inference delays d=0..4, not a single delay, so it is not directly comparable to per-delay (single-d) entries; stored in task_scores.reported_avg. Execution horizon s=4, 5 flow steps, 32 rollout episodes per level-delay tuple. Near-naive runtime.

Soft RTC (action-prior denoising), training-time variant: N=2 is the smoothness-oriented operating point (seed 1), lowering high-delay action delta/jerk relative to Train Hard RTC at some cost in solve rate (Liu et al. 2026, Table I). 12-task RTC protocol on the released large Kinetix levels (state-based). Reported solve rate is the overall mean averaged across inference delays d=0..4, not a single delay, so it is not directly comparable to per-delay (single-d) entries; stored in task_scores.reported_avg. Execution horizon s=4, 5 flow steps, 32 rollout episodes per level-delay tuple. Near-naive runtime.

Asynchronous-inference baseline reproduced in the REMAC paper: the pretrained flow-matching policy executes actions from the most recently generated chunk, with no test-time correction. 12-task RTC protocol; suite scores are mean success rate over the 12 tasks at inference delay d=0..4; for each d, execution horizon e is averaged within max(1,d) to 9-d. Scores at different d are not directly comparable.

12-task RTC protocol. Suite scores are mean success rate over the 12 tasks at inference delay d=0..4; for each d, execution horizon e is averaged within max(1,d) to 9-d. Flow-matching policy with LoRA (rank 4) fine-tuning and prediction horizon P=8. Scores at different d are not directly comparable.

REMAC combined with the Bidirectional Decoding (BID) test-time sampling method. 12-task RTC protocol. Suite scores are mean success rate over the 12 tasks at inference delay d=0..4; for each d, execution horizon e is averaged within max(1,d) to 9-d. Scores at different d are not directly comparable.

REMAC combined with the RTC (Real-Time Chunking) inpainting test-time method. 12-task RTC protocol. Suite scores are mean success rate over the 12 tasks at inference delay d=0..4; for each d, execution horizon e is averaged within max(1,d) to 9-d. Scores at different d are not directly comparable.

Third-party reproduction by the VLS paper. Non-standard subset: only task and position perturbations across 4 suites (8 of 20 core cells); 20 episodes per task (vs. 50 standard). overall_score omitted.
Third-party reproduction by the BayesVLA paper. Non-standard subset: only LIBERO-Object suite (4 of 20 core cells); object_obj not reported. overall_score omitted.
Third-party evaluation by the LIBERO-Pro paper. All 4 suites × 5 core perturbations plus env. 50 episodes per task. env cells kept out of the 20-cell mean.
Third-party reproduction by the GLaD paper. 19 of 20 core cells reported; spatial_task is missing (shown as dash). 50 episodes per task. overall_score omitted because of the missing cell.

First-party result from the GLaD (Geometric Latent Distillation) paper. 19 of 20 core cells reported; spatial_task is missing (shown as dash). 50 episodes per task. overall_score omitted because of the missing cell.

Third-party reproduction by the "See Less, Specify More" (S2) paper, used as the π₀.₅ baseline. Independent numbers (differ from the LIBERO-Pro paper's π₀.₅). Non-standard subset: only obj/pos/sem/task perturbations (no ori) on the goal, object, and spatial suites; the long (libero_10) suite is excluded. overall_score omitted.

Third-party reproduction by the AFI (Affordance Field Intervention) paper. Non-standard subset: only spatial and object suites with position perturbation (2 of 20 core cells); only 7 of 10 spatial tasks evaluated. overall_score omitted.
Third-party reproduction by the BayesVLA paper. Non-standard subset: only LIBERO-Object suite (4 of 20 core cells); object_obj not reported. overall_score omitted.
Third-party evaluation by the LIBERO-Pro paper. All 4 suites × 5 core perturbations plus env. 50 episodes per task. env cells kept out of the 20-cell mean.

Third-party reproduction by the VLS paper using a LeRobot reimplementation of π₀.₅; used as the base policy for VLS's training-free steering. Non-standard subset: only task and position perturbations (8 of 20 core cells); 20 episodes per task. model_paper overridden to arxiv.org/abs/2504.16054 (π₀.₅) for consistency across π₀.₅ entries. overall_score omitted.

Cited baseline reported by the VLS paper. Non-standard subset: only task and position perturbations across 4 suites (8 of 20 core cells); 20 episodes per task (vs. 50 standard). overall_score omitted.

Third-party reproduction by the BayesVLA paper. Non-standard subset: only LIBERO-Object suite (4 of 20 core cells); missing goal/spatial/long suites and the object_obj perturbation. overall_score omitted.

Third-party evaluation by the LIBERO-Pro paper that introduces this benchmark. All 4 suites (goal, spatial, long, object) across all 5 core perturbations (ori, obj, pos, sem, task) plus env. 50 episodes per task. Position and task perturbations drive scores to 0%; env cells kept out of the 20-cell mean.

First-party result from the VLS paper. VLS is a training-free inference-time steering method applied on top of a frozen π₀.₅ (LeRobot) policy. Non-standard subset: only task and position perturbations across 4 suites (8 of 20 core cells); 20 episodes per task. overall_score omitted.
First-party result from the SimVLA paper ("A Simple VLA Baseline for Robotic Manipulation"). All 4 suites × 5 core perturbations. Zero-shot evaluation without additional fine-tuning on LIBERO-PRO.

First-party result from the QuoVLA (Quotient Space for VLA) paper; built on a π₀.₅ backbone. All 4 suites × 5 core perturbations (ori, obj, pos, sem, task). Zero-shot evaluation of the LIBERO-trained policy.

First-party result from the "See Less, Specify More" (S2) paper; S2 is a planner-executor VLA on a π₀.₅ executor, here with the off-the-shelf Kimi K2.5 planner. Non-standard subset: only obj/pos/sem/task perturbations (no ori) on the goal, object, and spatial suites; the long (libero_10) suite is excluded. 50 episodes per task. overall_score omitted.

First-party result from the AFI (Affordance Field Intervention) paper; AFI applied on top of a π₀.₅ backbone. Non-standard subset: only spatial and object suites with position perturbation (2 of 20 core cells); only 7 of 10 spatial tasks evaluated. overall_score omitted.

First-party result from the BayesVLA paper (Bayesian factorization of visual-action prior and language-conditioned likelihood). Non-standard subset: only LIBERO-Object suite (4 of 20 core cells); object_obj not reported. overall_score omitted.

First-party result from the BayesVLA paper; R1 variant pre-trains both prior and likelihood. Non-standard subset: only LIBERO-Object suite (4 of 20 core cells); object_obj not reported. overall_score omitted.

First-party result from the APT (Action Expert Pretraining) paper; the "Ft VLM" variant jointly finetunes the VLM (vs. the frozen-VLM APT variant). Non-standard subset: only position and task perturbations across all 4 suites (8 of 20 core cells). overall_score omitted.

Reproduction by the Structural Latent Points paper (Jiang et al. 2026) of MultiMAE (Bachmann et al. 2022). Non-standard 6-task ManiSkill2 set (PickCube, StackCube, TurnFaucet, Hang, Pour, Fill); not the standard 5-task set. OBSBench evaluation framework with ACT policy; backbones pretrained on ScanNetV2.
Retrained by the E0 authors. Non-standard 5-task set (PegInsertionSide, PickCube, StackCube, PlugCharger, PushCube).
Retrained by the E0 authors. Non-standard 5-task set (PegInsertionSide, PickCube, StackCube, PlugCharger, PushCube).

Cited baseline in the R3D paper; Diffusion Policy (Chi et al. 2023) with an SPuNet sparse point-cloud encoder. Non-standard task set (PickCube, StackCube, PegInsertionSide Grasp/Align/Insert).
Retrained by the E0 authors (failed to solve any task). Non-standard 5-task set (PegInsertionSide, PickCube, StackCube, PlugCharger, PushCube).

Non-standard task set: PickCube, StackCube, and PegInsertionSide split into 3 subtasks (Grasp, Align, Insert); not the standard 5-task ManiSkill2 set. 1000 demos per task. Paper-reported aggregate (55.2) stored in task_scores.reported_avg.
Standard 5-task ManiSkill2 set. Trained from scratch on ~40K trajectories with 20 random camera views per trajectory; novel camera view generalization protocol. Averaging method: arithmetic.

Same 5 standard tasks but evaluation uses novel camera views from a 300K random camera pool (non-standard), so overall_score is null. Averaging method: arithmetic. Paper-reported aggregate (65.8) stored in task_scores.reported_avg.

Cited baseline from the GeoVLA paper. Standard 5-task ManiSkill2 set, arithmetic mean. Model paper set to the canonical Dita paper (2503.19757) to match other Dita entries; extract returned a different URL for this row.

Reproduction by the OC-VLA paper of Dita, retrained with robot-base data and a discrete action head. Standard 5-task ManiSkill2 set, arithmetic mean, 100 trajectories per task.
Retrained by the E0 authors. Non-standard 5-task set (PegInsertionSide, PickCube, StackCube, PlugCharger, PushCube).

Reproduction by the Structural Latent Points paper (Jiang et al. 2026) of Lift3D foundation policy (Jia et al. 2024). Standard 5-task ManiSkill2 set (PickCube, StackCube, PickSingleYCB, PickSingleEGAD, PickClutterYCB). Averaging method: arithmetic.

Reproduction by the MIKASA-Robo paper of π₀. Standard 5-task MIKASA-Robo protocol: 250 demos/task, 100 evaluation episodes/task at 128x128 RGB, end-effector control, averaged over 3 independent runs.

Reproduction by the MIKASA-Robo paper of the Decision Transformer baseline. Standard 5-task MIKASA-Robo protocol: 250 demos/task, 100 evaluation episodes/task at 128x128 RGB, end-effector control, averaged over 3 independent runs.

Reproduction by the MIKASA-Robo paper of the Recurrent Action Transformer with memory (RATE) baseline. Standard 5-task MIKASA-Robo protocol: 250 demos/task, 100 evaluation episodes/task at 128x128 RGB, end-effector control, averaged over 3 independent runs.

Generic Behavioral Cloning baseline (no specific source paper) reproduced by the MIKASA-Robo authors. Standard 5-task MIKASA-Robo protocol: 250 demos/task, 100 evaluation episodes/task at 128x128 RGB, end-effector control, averaged over 3 independent runs.

First-party result from the ELMUR paper. Standard 5-task MIKASA-Robo protocol with 3 independent training runs, 100 evaluation episodes per run with distinct seeds, paired RGB images (3x128x128) from static and wrist cameras, continuous 7-joint + gripper actions, binary sparse rewards.

First-party result from the μVLA paper (Cherepanov et al.); μVLA is a recurrent-memory LoRA fine-tune of OpenVLA-OFT (memory width m=64, TBPTT length K=2). overall_score is null: scores are from the non-standard 23-task MIKASA-Robo-VLA evaluation (Table 1), where the model is trained on a different 5-task mixture (RememberColor5, RememberShapeAndColor3x3, ShellGamePush, TakeItBack, InterceptMedium). Of the canonical 5 tasks, InterceptMedium (47) and RememberColor5 (93) are in-distribution while ShellGameTouch (0; trained on ShellGamePush instead), RememberColor3 (92) and RememberColor9 (41) are held-out generalization results — not comparable to standard-protocol rows. 100 evaluation episodes per task.

Reproduction by the MIKASA-Robo paper of the Diffusion Policy baseline. Standard 5-task MIKASA-Robo protocol: 250 demos/task, 100 evaluation episodes/task at 128x128 RGB, end-effector control, averaged over 3 independent runs.

Reproduction by the MIKASA-Robo paper of the Octo-small VLA baseline. Standard 5-task MIKASA-Robo protocol: 250 demos/task, 100 evaluation episodes/task at 128x128 RGB, end-effector control, averaged over 3 independent runs.

Reproduction by the MIKASA-Robo paper with K=4 action chunks. Standard 5-task MIKASA-Robo protocol: 250 demos/task, 100 evaluation episodes/task at 128x128 RGB, end-effector control, averaged over 3 independent runs.

Reproduction by the MIKASA-Robo paper with K=8 action chunks, fine-tuned. Standard 5-task MIKASA-Robo protocol: 250 demos/task, 100 evaluation episodes/task at 128x128 RGB, end-effector control, averaged over 3 independent runs.

Reproduction by the MIKASA-Robo paper of the Conservative Q-Learning baseline. Standard 5-task MIKASA-Robo protocol: 250 demos/task, 100 evaluation episodes/task at 128x128 RGB, end-effector control, averaged over 3 independent runs.

Reproduction by the MemoryVLA authors of CronusVLA on the standard 5-task MIKASA-Robo protocol: 250 demonstrations per task at 128x128 resolution, 100 evaluation episodes per task, end-effector control.

Reproduction by the MIKASA-Robo paper of SpatialVLA. Standard 5-task MIKASA-Robo protocol: 250 demos/task, 100 evaluation episodes/task at 128x128 RGB, end-effector control, averaged over 3 independent runs.
First-party result from the MemoryVLA paper. Standard 5-task MIKASA-Robo protocol: 250 demonstrations per task at 128x128 resolution, 100 evaluation episodes per task, end-effector control.

First-party result from the MemoryVLA++ paper (Shi et al.), a memory-and-imagination extension of MemoryVLA. Standard 5-task MIKASA-Robo protocol: 250 demos/task at 128x128 RGB, end-effector control, 100 evaluation episodes/task, all 5 tasks jointly trained for 20k steps, single input frame per forward pass. Per-task scores and 44.4 average read from Table III; protocol confirmed standard (5-task, not the 4-task ELMUR variant) from the paper body, resolving the extract-stage 'unknown' flag.

RoboCerebra paper's proposed Hierarchical Planning and Execution framework (Table 3). Hierarchical architecture (VLM planner + VLA controller) with memory module for dynamic monitoring and re-planning: GPT-4o planner + OpenVLA* (fine-tuned on RoboCerebra) controller. Evaluated on all 6 RoboCerebra dimensions over 600 rollouts. Not directly comparable with end-to-end VLA entries.

RoboCerebra paper's oracle upper bound (Table 4): ground-truth plan + OpenVLA* (fine-tuned on RoboCerebra) controller. Non-deployable; serves as planner-quality ceiling for the benchmark. Evaluated on all 6 RoboCerebra dimensions over 600 rollouts. Not directly comparable with end-to-end VLA or hierarchical entries; group separately as oracle.

Reproduction by the RoboCerebra paper (Table 3, labeled 'OpenVLA-Libero100' in the paper). End-to-end VLA architecture (no System 2 planner): OpenVLA pretrained / fine-tuned on Libero100 (not on RoboCerebra data), evaluated on all 6 RoboCerebra dimensions over 600 rollouts. Architecture is directly comparable with OpenVLA* but not with hierarchical entries.

Reproduction by the RoboCerebra paper (Table 3, labeled 'OpenVLA*' to denote fine-tuned on RoboCerebra data). End-to-end VLA architecture (no System 2 planner). Evaluated on all 6 RoboCerebra dimensions over 600 rollouts. Architecture is directly comparable with OpenVLA-Libero100 but not with hierarchical entries.

Reproduction by the RoboCerebra paper (Table 7, A.3 ablation on planners). Hierarchical architecture (VLM planner + VLA controller): Cosmos-Reason1-7B (specialized embodied VLM) planner + OpenVLA* (fine-tuned on RoboCerebra) controller. Evaluated on all 6 RoboCerebra dimensions over 600 rollouts. Not directly comparable with end-to-end VLA entries; controller-rooted bibkey follows the existing RoboCerebra hierarchical convention (Cosmos-Reason1 model paper not resolved by extract).

Reproduction by the RoboCerebra paper (Table 4 planner ablation). Hierarchical architecture (VLM planner + VLA controller): LLaVA-Next-Video-7B planner + OpenVLA* (fine-tuned on RoboCerebra) controller. Evaluated on all 6 RoboCerebra dimensions over 600 rollouts. Not directly comparable with end-to-end VLA entries; controller-rooted bibkey follows the existing RoboCerebra hierarchical convention (LLaVA-Next-Video model paper not resolved by extract).

Reproduction by the RoboCerebra paper (Table 3 main results / Table 4 planner ablation). Hierarchical architecture (VLM planner + VLA controller): GPT-4o planner (no closed-loop feedback; single-shot subgoal sequence) + OpenVLA* (fine-tuned on RoboCerebra) controller. Evaluated on all 6 RoboCerebra dimensions over 600 rollouts. Not directly comparable with end-to-end VLA entries; this is the 'Planner+OpenVLA*' baseline from the paper.

Reproduction by the RoboCerebra paper (Table 4 planner ablation). Hierarchical architecture (VLM planner + VLA controller): Qwen2.5-VL-7B planner + OpenVLA* (fine-tuned on RoboCerebra) controller. Evaluated on all 6 RoboCerebra dimensions over 600 rollouts. Not directly comparable with end-to-end VLA entries; controller-rooted bibkey aligns with the existing RoboCerebra hierarchical convention (extract resolved Qwen2.5-VL planner paper https://arxiv.org/abs/2502.13923, but the controller-rooted bibkey is preferred for cross-paper consistency).

Reproduction by the RoboCerebra paper (Table 7, A.3 ablation on planners). Hierarchical architecture (VLM planner + VLA controller): RoboBrain-2.0-7B (specialized embodied VLM) planner + OpenVLA* (fine-tuned on RoboCerebra) controller. Evaluated on all 6 RoboCerebra dimensions over 600 rollouts. Not directly comparable with end-to-end VLA entries; controller-rooted bibkey follows the existing RoboCerebra hierarchical convention (RoboBrain-2.0 model paper not resolved by extract).

Reproduction by the RoboCerebra paper (Table 7, A.3 ablation on planners). Hierarchical architecture (VLM planner + VLA controller): VeBrain-8B (specialized embodied VLM) planner + OpenVLA* (fine-tuned on RoboCerebra) controller. Evaluated on all 6 RoboCerebra dimensions over 600 rollouts. Not directly comparable with end-to-end VLA entries; controller-rooted bibkey follows the existing RoboCerebra hierarchical convention (VeBrain model paper not resolved by extract).

Perceptual memory via frame sampling, memory-as-context integration. π₀.₅ backbone; 512-token memory budget. Standard protocol: multi-task single model, 16 tasks × 50 episodes, max 1300 steps, 9-run mean (3 checkpoints × 3 seeds).
Perceptual memory via frame sampling, memory-as-expert integration (separate memory expert with block-wise causal attention). π₀.₅ backbone; 512-token memory budget. Standard 9-run mean.

Perceptual memory via frame sampling, memory-as-modulator integration (adaptive LayerNorm, cross-attention to memory). π₀.₅ backbone; 512-token memory budget. Best overall non-oracle model in MME-VLA suite. Standard 9-run mean.
Symbolic memory via grounded language subgoals generated prompt-only by Gemini-2.5-Pro (no fine-tuning). π₀.₅ low-level policy. Standard 9-run mean.
Symbolic memory with oracle (simulator ground-truth) grounded subgoals — non-deployable upper bound, not directly comparable to learned methods. π₀.₅ low-level policy. Standard 9-run mean.
Symbolic memory via grounded language subgoals from a fine-tuned Qwen3-VL-4B. π₀.₅ low-level policy. Standard 9-run mean.
Symbolic-memory baseline proposed in RoboMME: concatenates past actions with language tokens, inspired by UniVLA. π₀.₅ backbone. Evaluated over 3 runs (deviation from standard 9-run protocol).
Recurrent memory (RMT: Recurrent Memory Transformer), memory-as-context integration. π₀.₅ backbone; 512-token memory budget. Standard 9-run mean.
Recurrent memory (RMT), memory-as-expert integration. π₀.₅ backbone; 512-token memory budget. Standard 9-run mean.
Recurrent memory (RMT), memory-as-modulator integration. π₀.₅ backbone; 512-token memory budget. Standard 9-run mean.
Symbolic memory via simple (non-grounded) language subgoals generated prompt-only by Gemini-2.5-Pro (no fine-tuning). π₀.₅ low-level policy. Standard 9-run mean.
Symbolic memory with oracle (simulator ground-truth) simple subgoals — non-deployable upper bound, not directly comparable to learned methods. π₀.₅ low-level policy. Standard 9-run mean.
Symbolic memory via simple (non-grounded) language subgoals from a fine-tuned Qwen3-VL-4B. π₀.₅ low-level policy. Standard 9-run mean.
Perceptual memory via token dropping, memory-as-context integration. π₀.₅ backbone; 512-token memory budget. Standard 9-run mean.
Perceptual memory via token dropping, memory-as-expert integration. π₀.₅ backbone; 512-token memory budget. Standard 9-run mean.
Perceptual memory via token dropping, memory-as-modulator integration. π₀.₅ backbone; 512-token memory budget. Standard 9-run mean.
Recurrent memory (TTT: Test-Time Training), memory-as-context integration. π₀.₅ backbone; 512-token memory budget. Standard 9-run mean.
Recurrent memory (TTT), memory-as-expert integration. π₀.₅ backbone; 512-token memory budget. Standard 9-run mean.
Recurrent memory (TTT), memory-as-modulator integration. π₀.₅ backbone; 512-token memory budget. Standard 9-run mean.

Third-party reproduction by RoboMME authors. SAM2 backbone with memory bank (perceptual memory); predicts discrete keyframe actions executed by external motion planner. Best on ButtonUnmaskSwap due to reliable scripted execution. Evaluated over 3 runs (deviation from standard 9-run protocol).
Third-party reproduction by RoboMME authors. Memoryless baseline (conditions only on current observation) — no memory mechanism. Evaluated over 3 runs (deviation from standard 9-run protocol).

Third-party reproduction by RoboMME authors. Hybrid perceptual-symbolic memory: VLM (Qwen2.5-VL-7B high-level) infers language subgoals from accumulated keyframes, executed by π₀.₅ low-level policy. Strongest compared prior method. Evaluated over 3 runs (deviation from standard 9-run protocol).

Vanilla pi_0.5 baseline measured by the VLA-IAP authors. Reports 10 cherry-picked tasks, not the standard 6-track IS/PS system.
Non-standard task subset (5 select tasks) measured by the E0 authors; not the official 6-track IS/PS system.
pi_0.5 baseline measured in the A1 paper. Reports only 4 cherry-picked tasks (Toy, Fruit, Painting, Mahjong), not the standard 6-track IS/PS system.

π₀.₅ baseline in the GeCO paper. VLABench evaluation with Tracks 1-4 and Track 6 (unseen_texture) PS; Track 5 (cross_task) not reported. Paper-reported 4-track mean preserved in task_scores.reported_avg.

pi_0.5 retrained on VLABench official training data for 60K steps under the ACoT-VLA evaluation setup. Reports IS and PS across Tracks 1-4 and Track 6 (unseen_texture); Track 5 (cross_task) not reported. overall_score null because the paper's 5-track average does not match the standard Track 1-4 PS aggregation.

Original VLABench paper (2412.18194) predates the 6-track evaluation system. Uses legacy evaluation categories: visual generalization (seen/unseen x base/commonsense), language instruction generalization, unseen task generalization, and composite tasks. Retrained/evaluated by the VLABench authors.
OpenVLA baseline measured in the A1 paper. Reports only 4 cherry-picked tasks (Toy, Fruit, Painting, Mahjong), not the standard 6-track IS/PS system.

Original VLABench paper (2412.18194) predates the 6-track evaluation system. Uses legacy evaluation categories: visual generalization (seen/unseen x base/commonsense), language instruction generalization, unseen task generalization, and composite tasks. Retrained/evaluated by the VLABench authors.
Non-standard task subset (5 select tasks) measured by the E0 authors; not the official 6-track IS/PS system.

ERVLA, an embodied chain-of-thought VLA. Trained/evaluated on VLABench reporting PS and IS across Tracks 1-4 (In-dist, Cross Category, Commonsense, Instruction) and Track 6 (unseen texture); Track 5 (cross_task) not reported. overall_score null because the paper's reported average spans 5 tracks, not the standard Track 1-4 PS aggregation.

Evaluates VLABench as a VLM embodied reasoning benchmark (open-loop QA accuracy), not the standard VLABench 6-track manipulation evaluation with IS/PS metrics. Score is a VLM reasoning aggregate, not a manipulation Progress Score.

Evaluates VLABench as a VLM embodied reasoning benchmark (open-loop QA accuracy), not the standard VLABench 6-track manipulation evaluation with IS/PS metrics. Score is a VLM reasoning aggregate, not a manipulation Progress Score.

ETC (Embodied Trajectory-Coupled data) method, full configuration with action data + ETC co-training and Distribution Bridging; built on the Pi0-FAST framework. Reports VLABench Track 1 (in-distribution) and Track 2 (cross-category) PS/IS only; the other tracks are not reported, so overall_score cannot be computed.
Reports 5 cherry-picked selection tasks (Toy, Fruit, Painting, Poker, Mahjong) rather than the official 6-track IS/PS system. overall_score cannot be computed from this non-standard subset.
Reports only 4 cherry-picked tasks (Toy, Fruit, Painting, Mahjong) with 50 trials per task, not the standard 6-track IS/PS system.
GeCO applied to π₀ on VLABench with Tracks 1-4 and Track 6 (unseen_texture) PS reported. Track 5 (cross_task) not reported. Paper-reported 4-track mean (34.25) preserved in task_scores.reported_avg.

Table 2 reports Avg. PS = 51.1; Table 15 gives the Track 1-4 PS breakdown (In Distribution 67.8, Cross Category 25.1, Common Sense 48.2, Semantic Instruction 63.1). Mean of the four tracks matches the reported 51.1.

Trained on VLABench official training data for 60K steps with LLM backbone frozen. Reports IS and PS across Tracks 1-4 and Track 6 (unseen_texture). overall_score null because the paper's 5-track average does not match the standard Track 1-4 PS aggregation.
